display.purpose: Page-level artist name and section titles. One per page.

headline.purpose: App name in the global header. Not reused within pages.

body.purpose: Main content text — dates, venue names, descriptions. 65-75ch max.

label.purpose: Metadata: city, country, time, helper text. Minimum size in the system.

shadows.name: cta-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary CTA button. Small lift and accent halo.

shadows.name: form-container
shadows.purpose: Global search form. Separates form from header gradient.

motion.name: page-enter
motion.purpose: Page/content entrance. Reduced-motion fallback: instant show, no translate.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Button and input hover/focus state changes.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.purpose: Search form switches from column to row. Cards switch to 2-column grid.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.purpose: Show detail page switches to 2-column side-by-side layout.

components.name: Primary CTA Button
components.description: Full-width on cards. Links to show detail + setlist page.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; padding: 10px 12px; background: linear-gradient(to right, #0284c7, #2563eb); color: #fff; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; border-radius: 8px; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(2, 132, 199, 0.4); transition: background 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: linear-gradient(to right, #0ea5e9, #3b82f6); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #38bdf8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Disabled / Unavailable Button
components.description: Shows when no show link is available. Non-interactive.
components.css: .ds-btn-disabled { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; padding: 10px 12px; background: #1e293b; color: #64748b; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #334155; cursor: default; }

components.name: Event Card
components.description: Used in local discovery feed and artist/genre pages. Contains event name, date, venue, and CTA.
components.html:
Artist Name @ Venue

2026-07-15 • 8:00 PM

Crystal Ballroom • Portland, US

View Show + Setlist History
components.css: .ds-event-card { background: rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.6); border: 1px solid #1e293b; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; } .ds-event-card__title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: #f1f5f9; margin: 0 0 4px; } .ds-event-card__date { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #cbd5e1; margin: 0 0 2px; } .ds-event-card__venue { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #94a3b8; margin: 0; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Artist or genre text input. Part of the global search form.
components.css: .ds-input { flex: 1; background: #020617; border: 1px solid #475569; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #f1f5f9; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #64748b; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #38bdf8; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(14, 165, 233, 0.2); }

components.name: Search Form
components.description: Global search bar in the header. Contains mode select, value input, city input, and submit button.
components.html: <form class="ds-search-form"><select class="ds-select"><option>Artist</option><option>Genre</option></select><input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="Search artist"><button class="ds-search-submit" type="submit">Search</button></form>
components.css: .ds-search-form { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; background: rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.7); border: 1px solid rgba(51, 65, 85, 0.8); border-radius: 12px; padding: 12px; box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(8, 47, 73, 0.3); } @media (min-width: 768px) { .ds-search-form { flex-direction: row; align-items: center; } } .ds-select { background: #020617; border: 1px solid #475569; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #f1f5f9; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-select:focus { border-color: #38bdf8; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(14, 165, 233, 0.2); } .ds-search-submit { background: linear-gradient(to right, #0284c7, #2563eb); color: #fff; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(2, 132, 199, 0.4); transition: background 150ms ease; } .ds-search-submit:hover { background: linear-gradient(to right, #0ea5e9, #3b82f6); } .ds-search-submit:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Setlist Accordion Item
components.description: The signature component. Expands to show the song list for a historical show.
components.html:
• Song One
  • Song Two
  • Song Three
components.css: .ds-setlist-item { overflow: hidden; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #1e293b; background: rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.6); } .ds-setlist-toggle { display: flex; width: 100%; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 12px; padding: 12px 16px; text-align: left; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; } .ds-setlist-toggle:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03); } .ds-setlist-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #38bdf8; outline-offset: -2px; } .ds-setlist-date { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #f1f5f9; margin: 0 0 2px; } .ds-setlist-venue { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #94a3b8; margin: 0; } .ds-setlist-hint { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #94a3b8; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-setlist-body { border-top: 1px solid #1e293b; padding: 12px 16px; } .ds-setlist-songs { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-setlist-songs li { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #cbd5e1; }

narrative.overview:
The design system draws from the information environment of a real show: the whiteboard setlist backstage, the typed door list, the stack of tickets at the box office. Nothing decorative. Everything present because it has a job.

The system is a work in progress toward this north star. The current palette (cold slate + sky-blue) reflects the dominant SaaS dark-mode convention and is itself an anti-reference: design sessions should push the palette toward something with more earned character — warmer near-blacks, an accent that reads electric rather than corporate.

Density is deliberate. Music fans scan for specifics: dates, venues, names. The system uses tight spacing and small type where it works, opening up only where the hierarchy demands breathing room.
keyCharacteristics: Information-first: no decoration that doesn't carry meaning
Tactile and immediate: elements respond crisply; interactions feel direct
Fan-built, not corporate: language and layout feel like they were made by someone who goes to shows
Earned darkness: the dark theme references the venue environment, not a SaaS aesthetic
WCAG AA contrast at minimum for all text

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: PA Blue (#38bdf8) is the only accent color in normal flow. It appears on links, focus rings, and the header gradient only. The gradient on the primary CTA button is a deviation and should be resolved to a flat accent on future improvement passes.

rules.name: The Anti-Slate Rule
rules.body: The cold slate palette is documented as the current state, not the target state. Every design session should evaluate whether a given element can move toward more earned character. The dominant failure mode is 'looks like every other dark-mode tool' — if it does, that element needs attention.

rules.name: The No-Uppercase Rule
rules.body: Avoid uppercase tracking (e.g., tracking-[0.18em]) as a section kicker. The current header uses it; future passes should remove it. The venue board doesn't shout in caps-with-tracking.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: Card surfaces are flat. Shadows appear only on elements that require perceived lift: the primary CTA button, the global search form. Do not add shadow to content cards — that escalates decoration over signal.

dos: Do keep the background at Stage Blackout (#020617) or darker. The venue is never beige.
Do use text-slate-100 (#f1f5f9) for all headings and primary content text. Verify 4.5:1 contrast before any palette changes.
Do use rounded-lg (8px) consistently on all cards and interactive elements. Consistency is the system.
Do use focus:border-sky-400 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-sky-500/20 on every focusable input element. Keyboard navigation is not optional.
Do keep card surfaces flat (bg-slate-900/60, no shadow). Elevation is reserved for the CTA and search form only.
Do use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) for every animation.
Do favor type-driven hierarchy over icons and decorative elements. The information IS the design.
donts: Don't use the current slate-950 + sky-blue palette as a target. It is the anti-reference ('generic SaaS dark UI'). Use this DESIGN.md as a snapshot of current state, not an endorsement.
Don't use background-clip: text with gradient backgrounds on any text element. Gradient text is decorative and never meaningful.
Don't add border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or callouts.
Don't use uppercase tracking (e.g., tracking-[0.18em] uppercase) as a section kicker.
Don't build identical card grids with only icon + heading + body.
Don't replicate the Ticketmaster / StubHub pattern of competing CTAs and aggressive calls to action. One primary action per card, always.
Don't import Spotify's algorithmic discovery language — 'You might also like', 'Recommended for you'.
Don't add shadows to content cards. The Flat-by-Default Rule is not a suggestion.
Don't let placeholder text fall below 4.5:1 contrast. text-slate-500 on slate-950 is marginal — verify with any palette change.
